CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-26-2024 Continued To Mar-12-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
BORIS SORSHER, Esq., FISHER & PHILLIPS LLP (949) 851-2424 bsorsher@fisherphillips.com
Attorney for DefendantS GOLD DROP HOLDINGS, INC., SMD GLOBAL, INC., JOSEPH ENCINOSA, CHRISTOPHER WYATT, AND LIAM WAITE
No appearance by Plaintiff

Discussed, Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GOLD DROP HOLDINGS, INC., SMD GLOBAL INC., JOSEPH ENCINOSA, CHRISTOPHER WYATT, AND LIAM WAITE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer is overruled. Defendants say the complaint's Labor Code claims (counts 1-6) do not lie against individuals. (Memo. 5:11-13.) But Labor Code 581.1 provides that these particular claims lie against any "natural person who is an owner, director, officer, or managing agent of the employer" - which the individual defendants are pled to be. (Cmplt. 3:21-4:3.) The cases defendants rely on pre-date Labor Code 581.1. Whether unjust enrichment (count 9) is a cause of action is unsettled, but of no moment; any "double relief" issues can be addressed at trial. The elements of contract breach (count 10) are adequately alleged (Cmplt. 21:21-24:4); plaintiff need not "prove" her claim (Memo. 7:5) at the pleading stage. As to conversion (count 11), a complaint may plead alternative legal theories. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
BORIS SORSHER, Esq., FISHER & PHILLIPS LLP (949) 851-2424 bsorsher@fisherphillips.com
Attorney for DefendantS GOLD DROP HOLDINGS, INC., SMD GLOBAL, INC., JOSEPH ENCINOSA, CHRISTOPHER WYATT, AND LIAM WAITE

Discussed, Not ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS GOLD DROP HOLDINGS, INC., AND SMD GLOBAL INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants' motion to strike is denied. The motion is limited to Penal Code violations. (Not. 2:9-12; Cmplt. 19:20-20:7.) A criminal violation is a proper "unlawful" UCL predicate. A judge's "passions" are unlikely to be stirred (Memo. 2:15) and a jury typically does not see the complaint.

Order is signed.
